LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO'S MOTION FOR PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS SHOWN THAT IT IS LIKELY TO PREVAIL ON THE MERITS AND THAT THE RELATIVE INTERIM HARM TIPS IN ITS FAVOR. DEFENDANT IS ENJOINED FROM RAISING THE RENT ON THE LOW-INCOME UNITS PENDING FINAL DETERMINATION OF THIS ACTION, OR DISSOLUTION OF THE PRELIMINARY INJUNCTION. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: HAROLD E. KAHN; COURT REPORTER: LAVENA WARD CSR #7077

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jan-15-2013 Off Calendar

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Jan-23-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION is Off calendar. A stipulated injunction and order for entry of judgment was entered on November 6, 2012. Judge: Marla J. Miller; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185

MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Sep-13-2012 Continued To Jan-23-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Sep-19-2012 Continued To Jan-30-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Plaintiff City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction
Off Calendar Jun-01-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. JAMES EMERY, DEPUTY CITY ATTORNEY, TEL. NO. 554-4628 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER J. HEALEY OF MCKENNA LONG & ALDRIDGE LLP, TEL. NO. (619) 699-2491 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - THE MATTER WAS FIRST HEARD AT 12:05 P.M WITH A LUNCH BREAK AT 12:25 P.M. AND RESUMED FOR HEARING AT 1:30 P.M. THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. Plaintiff City And County Of San Francisco'S Motion For Preliminary Injunction is Granted. Plaintiff has shown that it is likely to prevail on the merits and that the relative interim harm tips in its favor. Defendant is enjoined from raising the rent on the low-income units pending final determination of this action, or dissolution of the preliminary injunction. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward CSR #7077 =302/HK
